reviews.review_description:
I love this game as it is really realistic as I said in the title. But the reason i gave it only 4 stars is because it is really complicated! Even though I had it for about a year now, I still can't figure out how to make it so i can change my Avatar. And it obviously says in the trailer that you can change the backround, AND YOUR AVATAR, and I still can't figure it out. Maybe I am just very bad at figuring stuff out, but again, this game is really complicated! I hate writing this bad review as it may seem like I do not like this game, but that is not true! I love Vermilion and i hope you don't think that I hate this game. I just want to write the downsides of this game, but everything thing else is AMAZING! I recomend this game!
In conclusion, that is why i love and somehow dislike this game.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks for your review! Unfortunately Meta recently decided to discontinue support for avatars in third party games, which is why they are no longer functional in Vermillion. This puts a big burden on me to make some kind of custom avatar system, which is in the works but as a solo developer, these things take time. Thanks for bearing with me!

reviews.review_description: You can see the brushstrokes, the weight of how you paint accurately affects the canvas, the light reflects off of the groves and the textures of not only the paint but the canvas itself, the painting palette is the most realistic and impressive I have seen in vr. Any size canvas, a large range of brushes, and any color you can imagine to mix, and if you don’t know what colors, there’s is a handy browser that you can follow tutorials along or find inspiration, HECK, trace too, this app is the best I am glazing it because it is that good, if you took your time to read this review I’m telling you this with all my ussy, BUT IT YOU WONT REGRET IT!!!!

developer_response.body: Hi Emoussy, Thomas here, the developer of Vermillion. Thank you very much for your glowing review!

reviews.review_description: In college. Have a bunch of textbooks I need to memorise. I found doing things with my hands that doesn't need reading or comprohending is really helpful for my retention. Been learning how to paint while listening to my textbooks and got a 97% on my organic chemistry exam. This app has been super helpful and fun simotaniously.

developer_response.body: Hi Liam, that's certainly a very curious use case and I'm super impressed with your results! Have fun in the studio, and keep acing those exams!

reviews.review_description: I haven’t made any art in years, used to be a full time artist. Dipped my toes back in with this app and really enjoying it! I love that I can have the browser open and listen to music while I paint - I do wish there were different kinds of paint as well, acrylic, watercolor etc. ink would be cool too

Vermillion VR is, without a doubt, one of the best creative apps available on vr. But I and many other artist i know almost always like to sketch out a rough composition or a basic outline before we commit to painting. An option to use a simple virtual pencil or charcoal stick would be so helpful in the game please think about making this an option🙏

The game is super fun and I love watching Yt in the background! However the controls take some time getting used to and my battery drained by 30% in half a hour😬  Not good for big paintings since your battery will die by then. Other wise the game is perfect! You can blend and paint to your hearts desires.

I’ve never been able to draw, but always wanted to try. Buying traditional supplies felt impractical, so I gave Vermillion a shot. You don’t physically feel the brush touching the canvas, but you adapt to it quickly. The toolset is genuinely complete: customizable brushes, canvases in almost any size, and a full color palette that lets you mix paints or just pick shades on the fly. For beginners or fans of guided projects, the paint-by-number templates are a fantastic way to learn. When you’re ready for total creative freedom, you can simply open a blank canvas and start from scratch. Features like undo, a ruler, and a solvent for blending make experimenting completely stress-free. If you want to explore painting without the physical mess or storage hassle, it’s a genuinely well-made experience.

developer_response.body: Hi there, Thomas here, the developer of Vermillion. Thank you very much for your kind review, I'm glad you're having such a good time in the studio!

reviews.review_description:
Imagine your own studio virtualized where you can work with oil paints with no mess.  Immerse yourself with a picture or a painting tutorial where you can learn to mix and apply colors and paint to a canvas.  

Without any formal training or having ever used oil paints in the real world, I was able to follow one of their tutorials to paint something (and it looks great, considering!)

Developers are active and helpful on their discord server, and artists are sharing their pictures too.  These pictures are incredible!!

Note:  the quest version here can use MR so you can see your own room.  The Steam version (not this version) allows you to paint within another PCVR app and use the tool.  I got both, it’s that good!

developer_response.body: Hi Rastyk, thank you very much for your review! Wishing you a great time at the easel!

reviews.review_description:
Amazing feel witho
ut the mess, but would love some dlc for brushes, pallet knifes etc.  Tapping brushes to produce foliage would be amazing!  Sponges too would be cool.

The app is okay but theres some things that are super annoying like how the only browser you can use is duck duck go. Google is so much better for the artist because it shows better images for someone to use, and theres more websites that can be easily accessed. The physics of painting is not that good as well. It makes no since when using a bristol brush that i have to enlarge my painting a lot just to paint in a somewhat tiny area. It shouldnt be hard for bristol brushes to put paint on canvas consistently. Another thing is that the lighting is darker overall so its hard to tell what your values are actually like. This is evident as well when exporting it because usually the values of the export and the values of the painting in the game or different. The paint in this game is also very flat. You cant get a lot of texture and variety because the paint is very one dimensional. Another thing is when you mess up and you try to go back a sizable amount it doesnt allow you to go back anymore after about 5 takebacks. There needs to be more brush variety because its pretty stale and thin paint needs to act and behave like paint with a good amount of paint thinner. And another thing is when thinning paint it dilutes it way too much and its super unrealistic and annoying because in real life you would still have tons of saturation when mixing paint thinner.

developer_response.body: Hi there! The browser is a browser, which can show any page. DuckDuckGo is the default search engine, from there you can go to any other page on the internet as you please, including Google. The reason why DuckDuckGo is the default rather than Google is because DDG allows you to open images in full screen directly, which can then be projected on the canvas. Regarding bristle brushes: I'm not fully understanding what you mean, but these are purposely bristly variants of the smooth brushes. If you want thick lines, you can switch to the smooth brush. I'll have to see what is possible regarding lighting. You can actually get very thick paint: first, make sure that the paint thickness slider is maxed out. Fully load the brush or palette knife on the palette by pressing it down on the palette and moving it around a few times. Then, apply light pressure on the canvas, as applying pressure on the canvas flattens the paint. You have more than 20 undos on the canvas, not 5. There are autosaves every few minutes so you can always go back to those. You can adjust how much thinner you use by only swirling the brush in the bucket a little before applying it to the palette. Hope that helps!

reviews.review_description: I came across this game from seeing a video of someone showing off their work. Super surprised at somethings it has, the overlay that can be used from photos and such is a neat feature. Along with the ability to watch videos while you play. The game itsself is super realistic, the painting and avle to blend like your really painting a canvas is awesome.  I just wish it had stencils, my hands get super shaky at times and I cannot make a circle or straight lines to save my life. I would even purchase an add on if it was available to have like a set of stencils in various shapes or stamps of shapes that could adjust to the size you want. I figured it would had already been a thing in the game but its not. But it is a great game for any artist to enjoy. My kid is a great artist so I cant wait to see what she can make with this game.

I used vermillion for a while and then reset my headset. I redownloaded it but it seems unable to determine that I paid. 

Please advise.

This has been resolved, thanks to thomas@vermillion-vr

I used to paint bob Ross painting many years ago and loved it and came about this app and wow it makes me want to paint again. But for now I will stick with this and get the feel of it again without all the mess.

developer_response.body: Happy to hear you're rediscovering your love for painting through Vermillion!

reviews.review_title: Most realistic painting app in VR
reviews.review_description: I haven’t had the game for very long and I’ve already painted like 5 masterpieces. Such a good game to just wind down with and create something cool. And the realism of paint mixing, brush strokes etc just adds to it.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review! Wishing you a great time in the studio.

reviews.review_description: I cant pass the tutorial, I just want  to play

developer_response.body:
Hi, sorry to hear you're having issues. If you follow the yellow highlighted instructions on the tutorial you'll pass it in a minute. Let me know if you need further instructions! Looking forward to your updated review once you're painting.

Update 03/02: I'm still awaiting your confirmation on whether you've been able to complete the tutorial. Thanks!

Update 23/03: Hi! I'd love to hear whether you've managed to complete the tutorial by following the yellow highlighted instructions. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: This game is perfect for an armature digital artist.

reviews.review_title: I'm a painter IRL. This app is truly exc
reviews.review_description: My favourite app. Love it so much. The realism of the paint physics, mixing, brushes and palette features are AMAZING. So many great features. It's incredibly relaxing too. Would love to see a version for watercolour or dry media. I'd definitely collect the set . Big respect to the developer

developer_response.body: Thank you for your glowing review! 🙌🏻

reviews.review_description: This is the best vr game I played with realistic stuff

Ive genuinely surprised myself with what I can accomplish by just looking at a reference photo and giving it a lot of time, attention to detail,   and patience. My only gripe I’ve had so far with my first 2 paintings is, how come I can only go back a small number of strokes? If I blend a portion of a painting, and I change my mind on how I want it blended, is the painting just screwed because I used a few too many strokes? I’m probably only complaining because I’m new, and I understand that in real life there’s no undo button, but since it’s there, why not let me back up all the way if need be?

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your review! You get 20 undo's. The reason there's a limit is because they require a lot of memory, and the Quest only has so much available. You can always quickly pop into the saves menu and save the painting in its current state before committing to changes you're not sure about. Hope that helps!

reviews.review_title: not for newbies dev needs to fix this
reviews.review_description:
every other art app i've tried has it so i can sample colors just by pointing at anything and then getting the color but this doesn't, why?

Seriously no excuse, imagine if other editors didn't have an eye dropper for getting the exact same color.

it also ignores disabilities what about people that have color issues.

20$ for an app i can't actually use unlike $5 and free apps

---

Reply 1
2/2

Thank you for actually responding, you have no idea what it's like WANTING to get into art but you have barriers and accessibility ALWAYS comes LAST, meaning people like me not getting into art despite wanting to desperately.

For instance, in the app VR Painter, I can have more than one canvas and I can import an image as a canvas, grab the color from it and just paint on the main canvas.

Your responses are geared towards elitism, you're going for super-realism which ignores accessibility.

In real life I can just get a color code and order that color of paint.
In real life I can draw physically and paint over that.
Or I can stencil and paint that.

I also don't see an option to dry a layer and make it wet again like in vr painter.

If I could do these things the app would be accessible to me like it is to base audience and I'd be able to paint endlessly like I've always wanted to.

I've been poor all my life and got lucky getting VR for therapy, I never got into painting because it's expensive to make mistakes - I wanted an option to paint like in real life, but digitally.

If I could do these things in your app then I'd get real brushing experience and could transfer that to real life without spending money on paint and space.

developer_response.body:
Reply 2/2 Hi again! Regarding the eye dropper, it will definitely be updated to allow sampling off images. That being said, don't be afraid to experiment with mixing colors, that's a big part of the fun! There's a video tutorial on mixing colors linked in the default bookmarks, top right button of the browser. As far as painting over something and making layers wet/dry: there are two options for this. First is the canvas mixing sensitivity slider, which is located on the palette so it's always readily available. If you set that to zero, you paint over anything without any mixing. The higher you set it, the more the color blends on the canvas. The other option is the layers from the Layers panel on the floating screen. Different layers don't mix with each other. You can merge them if you want to blend them together, or to free up more layers. Hope that helps!

Reply 1/2 Hi there, I'm sorry you're having troubles getting started. When you pick up the rag for the first time, you get a tooltip that explains that you dip the rag in the paint thinner to be able to erase fully. This is useful because it allows you to gently fade out paint when you want to, or fully erase it if you want that instead.

The eye dropper samples paint from the palette or the canvas, and in a future update it will also be able to do so from the browser. You don't need it for mixing paint, it's a convenience option for those that want it. Mixing paint is done by just mixing colors together on the palette. The starting palette has plenty of colors available to easily get whatever color you need. 

I would recommend using the paint by numbers generator. This will mix up the colors for you as well as giving the outlines for the painting.

reviews.review_description: Hi! Love the program. Latest update I can’t export/sync cloud to get my artwork. Please fix! I rely on this app for my designs. Thank you.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks! You still can, it's just that Meta broke the Open Files App button with their OS v81 update. The files are still exported to the gallery though, but until it fixed on Meta's side, you will have to open the app through the Quest menu.

reviews.review_description: There should be a way to delete a layer and pencils would be great.
Truly an amazing experience. Thank you for making this game 🙏

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your kind review!

reviews.review_description:
Genuinely relaxing and fun. Avoids a lot of discomfort I find in other painting games with hand placement. And holding brushes.
Enjoyed it a lot.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your kind review! - Thomas

reviews.review_description: Could you guys add a pencil, it would help a lot with sketching out ideas before painting.

developer_response.body: Hi, Thomas here, the solo developer of Vermillion. Thank you for your glowing review and your suggestion! Have you tried using the alternate rigger brush, the one you swap to after grabbing the long rigger brush from the rack? That has a short static tip. If you use that with some thinned paint and the infinite paint toggle enabled, you have yourself a sketching tool! If there is something I'm missing as to why you would like to see a pencil specifically, you can let me know on Discord: https://vermillion-vr.com/discord

reviews.review_description: I was hesitant at first but this app is incredible!!! Watching Bob Ross I thought I give it a go and it is superb. I have painted incredible paintings when I thought I couldn’t paint. After this app I bought acrylic paints and found the skill transfer very well into real life. Thank you so much!!!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review! I couldn't be happier to hear that you've picked up painting IRL because of Vermillion. Wishing you a lot of fun at both the virtual and real easel! - Thomas

reviews.review_description: This VR painting app is pretty cool, bur I would like a pencil and more layers to work with

developer_response.body:
Hi, Thomas here, Vermillion's solo dev. Thank you for your review and your suggestions! Have you tried using the secondary rigger brush? That has a short static tip. If you use that with some thinned paint and the infinite paint toggle enabled, you basically have a pencil! If there is something I'm missing as to why you would like to see a pencil specifically, you can let me know on Discord: https://vermillion-vr.com/discord

Regarding layers, remember you can merge layers, making them infinitely reusable. That's more organized than having a Photoshop style mess of layers! If you're enjoying the app, I would appreciate a rating that reflects that appropriately 🙏🏻

reviews.review_description: Without a doubt the best way to paint in VR . So easy and fun to paint simply amazing looking landscapes with beautifully blended color.

developer_response.body: I'm very happy to hear that, thank you! Have fun in the studio!

reviews.review_description:
I have absolutely no experience with painting, I just happened to grab this while it was on sale as it looked very well made. Little did I know I would end up loving this app! Possibly the first VR experience I truly lost track of time and just enjoyed the serene ambiance. I probably won’t get around to hauling painting supplies to the top of a mountain in my lifetime, this app makes it possible to bring that experience home and accessible to everyone.

If you’re even semi interested I’d say give this app a shot. I wasn’t really sure what to expect as I have no painting skill at all. There’s still something for everyone. There’s a paint by numbers mode that’s closer to a coloring book while you still learn to control brush strokes. There’s tutorial videos, paint a longs, and more to help get you started.

In short this is the perfect intro to the hobby in my opinion. Painting supplies can take up a lot of room, cost a lot of money, take time and effort to clean up, and just has a bigger barrier to entry than people realize. This has allowed me to dip my feet into the hobby without having to break the bank or feeling overwhelmed.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your glowing review!

reviews.review_description: All the great fun of painting without the mess ! So realistic!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your glowing review! Wishing you a lot of fun on the canvas. - Thomas

reviews.review_description: The brush pressure is not realistic. Some large brushes are like wood that doesn't bend at all when you apply pressure. Painting VR has very realistic pressure next to it. This should be taken as a lesson and improved.

developer_response.body: Not every brush is a mop, some real life brushes have stiff bristles. This is what Vermillion simulates.

reviews.review_description: I’m not much of a artist but this is becoming a nice unwind thing, my only request is that you add a pen-pencil to draw a picture before painting and if possible include paint tubes and use of fingers

reviews.review_title: READ IF YOU ARE CONSIDERING PAINTING VR
reviews.review_description:
If you are deciding between this and painting vr, this is for you, i bought both and painting vrs physics are really not great, only brush physics, which are just as good in vermillion. yes there is spray paint, large brushes and that sort of stuff in painting vr, but its really not very good. its really hard to get used to painting vrs controls but in vermillion its all very easy to understand and do. of course painting vr has the mechanic of rubbing your hand over the canvas to mix paint. thats really cool but theres a lil rag in vermillion that you can use to create blends in the painting. if you want to do grafitti, go for kingspray, if you want grafitti with some painting, go painting vr (however dont expect a realistic painting experience), if you want a realistic painting simulation, go vermillion. Another thing to note is that in painting vr all the brushes you would realistically need to make a half decent painting need 1 or 2 dlcs, which is more of your money towards the developer. one of the dlcs is "esencialls brush pack" which is exactly as described, esencial brushes to make any actual bit of art😅. like ive already just paid £15 for the game, now you want more so i can play it properly? no thanks. painting vr was a very quick refund for me, but vermillion was spot on from the start. 


REVIEW: absolute top tier painting experience, great physics, love the mixing, cleaning, adjusting the way you hold your brush, its all very well thought out. the browser is pretty good aswell, although the keyboard does not toggle on certain websites, its only really their for youtube tho so thats fine. love this game

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your thorough review!

reviews.review_description: I really love this app! It is nice to be able to paint without the mess or smell. I don’t have to worry about wasting supplies. There are a few things that would elevate it though. I’d like to see a pencil that has the ability to use feather light strokes, the ability to make perfect circles, a sponge, a small round brush for flower petals, a wire brush for scratching, a tiny blender brush, and a fine tip eraser. I would also really love it if we could hide and unhide layers as well. Please keep updating this app. It is a keeper!

I love this app, I have been using it for a while now and the paint by number is my favorite. However a lot of times it changes the colors of the original reference picture. Hope to see a fix for that.

developer_response.body: Hey there, happy to hear you're enjoying the ability to generate your own paint by numbers templates! By nature, paint by numbers has to compress the millions of colors of the reference image into just a few colors, currently at most 20. The algorithm picks the most common colors in the image, but it can't detect small distinct color areas that are obvious to our eyes. Remember that you can always still mix up your own colors by changing the palette from the palette menu. In a near future update, the eye dropper tool will also be able to sample colors directly from the source image, making the process even simpler. Hope that helps!

reviews.review_description: I started with Paint VR. Don't get me wrong I love it. The ease of creating a painting is a snap there. But it just doesn't possess the sheer depth of mixing and even how you can hold your brush, nor knives like this has. I'm only just learning. I'm loving the creative options I have before me. Certainly I've a lot to learn about the mixing slider. Difference in pressure etc. Controls aren't over complicated. And ultimately your creativity is truly your limit here. If you can think it, and visualize it, you can make it so. If you've wanted to try your hand at actually painting but don't want the mess. This is absolutely worth the 20$ I paid.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your glowing review, enjoy your time in the studio! If you want to trade tips and share your art with the community, you're always welcome to join at https://vermillion-vr.com/discord/

reviews.review_title: If you trying to paint yall own pictures
reviews.review_description:
Without PC search in YouTube my channel : QuestOG 

I just posted a 2 minute  tutorial video am not trying to flex but no one else has done this method before 😎 amazing game btw so relaxing

I am not an artist but I enjoy the idea of being able to express myself and being able to produce a piece of art. Following the video tutorial for the Robin picture blew me away. I was filled with pride and accomplishment. Thank you for making this. To everyone reading this who are thinking about buying it, don't think, just buy. To Meta, get this app on Quest+ quickly for all members to be able to experience this fantastic work.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review!

reviews.review_description: I'm an oil painter. I had hoped this would be a relaxing way to paint casually without all the clean up. The paint and the brushes just don't behave like reality at all. I hadn't expected much as it's a video game, but I was surprised how unpleasant the experience was.

developer_response.body: I'm sorry to hear your expectations haven't been met. As you can see from the other reviews, most people, including painters, do find great enjoyment with the app. I'd ask you to give it a bit of time to adjust to the software, rather than shooting it down based on your first expectations. Anything different from something you are deeply familiar with always requires some time to adjust.

reviews.review_title: The best app ever!! A Gem! Get it!!
reviews.review_description:
I don't think the creators of this app are aware of how much good impact this app makes  in the lives of people. Young , old, sick , well..   It is the closest to real life painting and it actually  motivates you and helps you for real life painting in many ways 

1 You can create a fast palette  for your real life painting by using the color by numbers features
2.  You can transfer your painting  skills to real life
3. It has youtube built in , you can follow painting  tutorials 
4. You can print out your paintings and sell them
5. Paint parties and classes
these are just a few ways it helps 

My sister has very very bad eyesight and because  of that she has not been able to finish a painting in years And since I gifted her this one she is so happy that she can get so close to the canvas or stretch it big and she's finished two paintings in just 3 days 

Fantastic for the elderly,  and mentally  sick,  for artist and aspiring artist.   Sadly the upgrade that cause  the removal  of avatars has put a slight dent on this game.  

I am so much praying the developer  brings it back and considers it plays a very important  part of the game  which is not really a game this as I call it is a true *gem* 

 The avatars back will make it perfect! 

For one my sister  can't see well the avatar can help with that.. so it does help the impaired  like my sister 

also makes the experience  of multi-player much more feel real , I felt that when I had my sister over in my living room , it somehow makes you feel close  .  

Imagine what that can do for others too they can paint with their family's  and feel somewhat their presence is there together with them..

A vr paint party would be so much better with your own individual  avatars.   
In my opinion the game is beyond perfect and the avatars will make it complete and thrive even more

I'm trusting that all this will be put into thought and even just for the few that have the app bring the avatars back and make this game 1000% perfect again.   

You whomever created this game have undervalued yourself and not given enough credit and appreciation  for what you've actually  created!  This is Gold! A gem! A gift !  Not to be slepted  on!  Keep it as such pls it will bless you and your hard work will be so worth it not only for you but for many many others! 

Bring those avatars back I pray!  I Thank you!  Bless you for this creation! 

 Vermillion Vr is the true masterpiece!

reviews.review_title: The best app on Meta Quest
reviews.review_description:
This is my first review on the Meta store. 
Undoubtedly, Vermillion is the best  app on Meta Quest. 

As someone with very basic artistic skills—essentially just stick figures, I've always wanted to try painting. However, the thought of the complex setup and the potential mess in my room always deterred me. Vermillion provides a perfect solution, allowing me to start painting immediately and unconditionally. 
With tutorials recorded by the developers, I can quickly create an oil painting, even though my skills are still very limited. This has been a satisfying experience. 

Additionally, the developer highly values community feedback. During use, I encountered an issue with unresponsive browser clicks, which, after communicating with the developer on Discord, was quickly resolved in a new version. 

Overall, Vermillion is an absolutely worth-trying virtual painting app.

developer_response.body: Very happy to hear that, that's what I made Vermillion for! And thanks for coming onto Discord to provide feedback, I'm glad the issue is resolved with an easier to use browser as a result.

reviews.review_title: Pretty fun and relaxing but blurry.
reviews.review_description:
This app is pretty cool. It is fun and relaxing. I love being able to paint without the mess. I love being able to virtually paint in passthrough, so I can see my kids running around without fear of them spilling any paint.
  However, it did take a while to figure out how to import a custom paint by number photo. Also, I’m not sure if this is because it’s not caught up in development yet but I’m finding the resolution to be a little blurry in quest 3. I have to take constant breaks cause my eyes start hurting. The canvas is not as bad as the browser. I can barely make out words on the in-game browser because it is too blurry.

Reply: I actually don't have an issue with the canvas. That is good and clear. It's when I use the browser, even to watch tutorials, it is blurry for some reason.

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your review! Very happy to hear passthrough being put to good use! Regarding the blurriness: the canvas in Vermillion is the sharpest anything can be in VR. I've gone to great lengths to implement the latest available technology to display something with perfect clarity in VR. If you are still experiencing it as blurry, make sure your IPD is set up correctly and the headset is positioned correctly. If you wear prescription glasses, it could be beneficial to look into Quest 3 prescription lenses if you're planning on using your headset often. I am planning on giving the browser the same treatment in the near future, though it is mostly only required for seeing images or videos, not for reading. But I can definitely appreciate the need for ultimate clarity when typing in a search query, so keep an eye out for that in the coming weeks. Hope you have fun in the studio!

reviews.review_description: I want to start this review by saying I bought the Meta Quest specifically for this app! As a painter turned mom of three, this app is the only fortress of solitude I get. I feel it's pretty realistic and intuitive for real painting. If anything, it keeps my muscle memory strong and allows me to practice when I'm unable to use actual art supplies or have the space. I want to give a shout-out to the amazing developers for creating this app. I'm excited to see its future and other possibilities!!

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you very much for your kind review, it really means a lot! I wish you a great time in the virtual studio. You're very welcome to tag @vermillionvr on Instagram if you want to share a piece!

reviews.review_title: I am unable to complete the setting stag
reviews.review_description: I am stuck at the X button stage when the wizard box asks you to clean. I pressed the X button but it didn't take me to the next stage of settings.

developer_response.body: Let me know if you got this sorted! The new tutorial skips this step entirely, as the button binding is shown on the palette.

reviews.review_description:
In fact, I love the painting, and the fun of painting in VR. But one thing I really hate about your app. The fact that you limit my choice of browser! I hate Duck Duck Go, it is absolute crap. And the way it is in your application, I can't even use URL's to go to my own websites.
I use it on a Metaquest 3, Where I have no option to set my foto's to your map. So I cant paint the things I want to paint, only pictures I find with your absolute crappy browser! Change it, so I can use URL's and can give you 5 stars! This is an absolute thumbs down for me! If I was able to give no stars at all I would have done that, that how I hate the browser part!
T
he fact of the quik response, and the ensurance thay are working on a version with URL inputmade me change from one to 3 stars. Like already told the program on itself is worth more then 5 stars. When the URL option is there, I wil change my review completely.

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks for your very raw feedback. DuckDuckGo is a search engine like Google, not a browser. With it you can go to any website you search in it. You should also be able to find your own site that way. That being said, entering URLs manually will come in a future update. You can already use images you copy over USB directly, by clicking the folder icon above the browser. That's probably going to be the easiest way for you to use your own photos. If you need more assistance with that, let me know in Discord (https://vermillion-vr.com/discord) or by mail, support [at] vermillion-vr.com. Looking forward to hearing from you!

reviews.review_description: Its great. Wish there were more backgrounds to paint in

I was super excited to use this.  The reviews made it sound phenomenal.  Sadly, the actual use is clunky, glitchy, and frustrating.  It kind of works for the paint by number, but if you're hoping to actually have the behavior of oil paints accurately simulated, you're in for a huge disappointment.  Inconsistent strokes and mixing, glitchy movements that suddenly smear random colors across your canvas.  A terrible interface.  Probably one of the worst painting apps I've used in VR.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your feedback. I'm sorry to hear about your experience, as it seems quite different from the overwhelmingly positive reviews Vermillion has received. I'm eager to understand your concerns better and address any issues you've encountered. Could you please provide specific examples or screenshots so I can investigate and improve your experience? Your input is valuable, and I'm committed to making Vermillion the best it can be for all users. You can reach me at support [at] vermillion-vr.com.

reviews.review_description: The quality of the graphics is terrible. The selection of spaces is also very small. It should sell for 5 dollars at most.

developer_response.body: Hi! I'm sorry to hear this isn't the app for you. Vermillion is a highly realistic painting simulation. It is not a travel app with many destinations. The graphics are exactly as shown on the store. The quality of the canvas is unmatched in VR.

reviews.review_description: It works perfectly. Painting works great. Mixing paint works exactly how you would expect real paint to mix. It's just amazing. My only question is about the purpose of the empty brush slot. I think I would like to have a pencil to make outlines or some charcoal brushes added in. Other than that, it's perfect.

I really enjoy the aesthetics of the app, and want to spend more time. However, I get a bit frustrated with 2 issues- the first is that I can’t seem to get a natural grip on the brushes; I pull the trigger over and over while trying to adjust my grip, but to no avail. The other issue is that- once I pick up the palette, I can find no way to put it down again. Is there a trick to it? No one else seems to mention this. If it weren’t for these two issues, I would give this app 5 stars.

developer_response.body:
You're using the wrong trigger - it's the grip button to grab and drop things, and to reposition the brush, not the trigger on the front. You can drop the palette when the big yellow highlight appears when you hold the palette on the palette stand. Hope that helps! Otherwise, come into Discord for support: https://vermillion-vr.com/discord

Looking forward to hearing back from you!

reviews.review_description: I've never painted before, ever!! As soon as I started this I was painting in no time. I played a Bob Ross YouTube video next to the painting and it turned out great and so much fun!! The detail and realism is fantastic. It is really very well developed and I can't wait for my VR to recharge so I can paint some more!! I'd give it 10 stars and very, very much worth the money!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the glowing review, have fun in the studio!

reviews.review_description: Excellent experience! Lobe that you can watch tutorials while you paint so you can learn

I like this game alot, it's very cool having my own art studio but I would love to have the option to change the type of paint I can use. I don't know how to paint with oil in real life and it's not any easier in vr. I'm an watercolor and acrylic painter and would love to be able to use those materials in vr!!!.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for your feedback! I always love to hear from fellow artists. Vermillion only has one paint type due to the unique characteristics of wet-on-wet painting and my quest to truly capture that experience to a T (and create some beautiful transitions... you can even pull and blur the paint!) . The best part is ... because of this dedication to a classic paint style, it could be the perfect opportunity for you to learn a 3rd style. There are some great YouTube tutorials out there to help you with the differences between acrylics and oils. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uSkGCayNwqk I can't wait to see what you create!

Edit 10/5: Hi! I'd love to know if the tutorials helped you get a better understanding of painting in Vermillion. Please feel free to join the Discord community at https://vermillion-vr.com/discord or the Facebook community at https://facebook.com/groups/vermillionvr to get lots of advice from your fellow painters.

reviews.review_description: Just amazing!

reviews.review_title: The Joy of painting at such a price
reviews.review_description:
I've never painted before, and found myself following Bob for my first painting in two 1h sessions. The results were amazing and the emotion of joy and peace this bringed is invaluable. 
My only complain is on standalone version there is no 4k/8k but thats hardware limitation :)
I think this is a must, lovely. Gz.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! The resolution on standalone is lower than on PC, simply because a standalone device can't handle the same resolution of the oil painting simulation as a PC can. Vermillion is cross buy, so you get the Rift version for free, if you want a higher resolution result (you have to unlock it in the store to get it). You can project your own images, by simply uploading them to Drive or Dropbox or a similar service, and logging into the browser to get them.

reviews.review_description: A pencil or something that you can draw a outline with and maybe a feature for colour blind and maybe a colour dropper so you can add more colour to a mix your making without messing it up to much but this game is amazing as soon as I got it I spent 4 hours just making colours

I am not a painter. Hell, I’m not even an artist of any kind, but I love this app. It’s super relaxing. There’s no one to judge my painting. I can just have fun…like when I was a kid before the guardrails went up. I love the occasional rain sounds in the environments, too. It took me one or two times to get the hang of some of the controls, but there’s always an undo option. Good thing. Even though the canvases are unlimited, I don’t think it’s good to get into the habit of throwing them out the window (nope, not really an option). You will get the hang of this too. Oh, I’m actually not sure if this has to be a solo venture, but eventually it might be nice to be able to paint with friends. Is that/will that be an option? Great job with this! I love it.

I love drawing and I’ve recently got into painting with acrylic gauche and I love it… just HATE the mess. Plus I don’t have a whole lot of space in my house to keep/set up art supplies either. 

My sister gave me her Oculus and this just so happened to pop up when I was playing around with the store. 

This is such a well thought out simulation and I can tell it was made with a lot of care and passion for artists.
It’s a pretty simple set up but it gets the job done. And all the little quirks you can’t do in real life, like use layers or undo, is a nice modern touch. 
The canvas/supplies/paints are very realistic and I feel like a real painter when using them. Plus the little studio atmospheres are fun and pretty relaxing. Really gets you in the zone. 

I don’t have any complaints about the game really and the dev is adding new things all the time and is super active on discord. Can’t wait to use it more!

If you are an artist, get Vermilion. If you’re a beginner or just wished you could try painting without buying hundreds of dollars in paint, easel, canvas or you just don’t have the space in your place for a studio, get Vermilion. If you don’t have an artistic bone in your Art loving body, you know the drill, get Vermilion! If you wish you had your own instructor, there are even super tutorials included. Vermillion is simply THE most exciting painting app to hit the planet. You will be impressed, you will be delighted, and best of all, YOU WILL HAVE SO MUCH FUN! 5⭐️ Fun!

reviews.review_title: Love it! Having 1 issue tho please read!
reviews.review_description:
Man this app is so good!!  Absolutely love it!!

When I take off the headset and put it back on, it puts me right on top of my canvas.  I have to try to look behind me and reach over my shoulder to point at the ground behind me just to move myself back. Sometimes that’s a challenging thing to do. If its got to reset me somewhere, please make it away from my canvas not on top of it.

developer_response.body: To reset the easel where it belongs, long press the menu button (flat button on the left controller). Recenter yourself with the long press Oculus button.
